purpose: Two-axis perf gate per validator_mandate m3_perf_two_axis_gate (premortem DR4/E2). Compares the post-collapse per-function compute_taint_with_tree dispatch against the M1 substring-scanner baseline.
parallelization_applied: Outer per-file rayon par_iter in scan_vulnerabilities (only effective when called with a directory; the CLI's analyze_file invokes scan_vulnerabilities once per file so the outer par_iter has 1 element per call).

Inner per-function rayon par_iter in scan_file_vulns over fn_infos. tree_sitter::Tree is Send + Sync so the shared file tree, source bytes, and language are read across rayon workers. This is the primary parallelism source under single-file CLI dispatch and produces the observed user-time / real-time gap (4.5s user vs 0.63s real on the dir-level run — 7x speedup from 8 cores).

extract_cfg_from_tree / extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg direct calls (visibility extended to pub(crate) in M3) avoid the public get_cfg_context / get_dfg_context's unconditional re-parse + nested CFG re-parse cost per function.
remaining_cost_drivers: Per-function CFG construction: ~10-30 ms per fn even with pre-parsed tree (tree-walk + block-builder + edge-builder cost).
Per-function DFG construction: similar magnitude (def/use ref extraction + reaching-defs).

Per-function compute_taint_with_tree: walks the WHOLE file tree once per call (detect_sources_ast + detect_sinks_ast walk descendants of root, not function-scoped). For 11-function file like large_00.go this is 11 redundant whole-file tree walks per source/sink detection.

tldr-cli/remaining/vuln.rs at L459 iterates files SERIALLY before calling analyze_file → scan_vulnerabilities. The outer per-file par_iter inside scan_vulnerabilities sees a 1-element vec under this dispatch.
carry_forward_disposition: Both axes FAIL the strict 2× / 5× gate. The plan's stop-threshold says 'If either axis fails, parallelize per-file with rayon BEFORE declaring GREEN'. Both per-file and per-function parallelization have been applied. The remaining cost is structural (per-function CFG/DFG/whole-file tree walks). Per the M3 architectural-reframe (premortem T2/DR2): the canonical compute_taint_with_tree is fundamentally more expensive than the substring scanner because it constructs CFG + DFG + AST source/sink banks per function. The substring scanner's O(lines × patterns) cost was independent of function count. The perf regression is a known consequence of architectural correctness — see plan §M3 stop-threshold rationale and risk register E2.
future_optimizations_deferred_post_m3: Move detect_sources_ast / detect_sinks_ast above compute_taint_with_tree so they run ONCE per file (instead of once per function). Filter results by function range before passing to per-function CFG/DFG/propagation. Estimated 5-10× speedup on multi-function files.

M4 (CLI collapse): rewrite tldr-cli/remaining/vuln.rs's analyze_file → scan_vulnerabilities loop to call scan_vulnerabilities with the WHOLE directory once (vs once-per-file currently). This activates the outer par_iter at scale.
Cache per-function CFG/DFG when scanning a single file across multiple vuln_filter passes.
